title: Lepisorus zippelii
taxonomy: Lepisorus zippelii is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the polypody fern family. This species belongs to the genus Lepisorus, which consists of several species of epiphytic ferns. Lepisorus zippelii is native to Southeast Asia and is also known as the Zippel's lip fern. Further classification within the family and genus may include specific orders and subgroups based on its characteristics and evolutionary relationships. The exact classification may vary depending on the source and the most recent taxonomic research.

Traditionally, Lepisorus zippelii has been used in herbal medicine to alleviate various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
